texts.full_text: areas of development Port Areas The Aqaba Port Areas include the Main Port, the Container Port and the Southern Industrial Port. According to the Master Plan, a common location—the Main Port—is established to merge the three existing port area activities into one expanded entertainment, residential, hotel and cruises service center. Coral Coastal Zone The Coral Coastal Zone is undergoing a wide residential, hotel and entertainment facilities construction process for the development of an advanced community resort, with respect to relative beach and coral reef protection requirements. Southern Industrial Zone The Zone extends along the Southern Industrial Zone Port and consists of reorganized and expanded current sites. New transportation systems, including a railway terminal, will serve the developed area. Airport Industrial Zone Ideal for industrial facilities, permitted land uses in the Zone include logistics and distribution, high-tech industries, warehousing, light manufacturing, showrooms, office complexes and airport-related business activities. The Master Plan aims at preserving natural resources within the construction frame. It comprises several zones and reserves for the protection of Aqaba's cultural, archaeological, historical and natural heritage and diversity. Areas include: Five environmental zones, coral reserves, archaeological reserves, natural area reserves and a Beach Protection Zone. Aqaba International Laboratories Aqaba International Laboratories - BEN HAYYAN was established under the EU funded programme “IS-ASEZA”, to support ASEZA in guiding the Aqaba Special Economic Zone towards becoming a dynamic and attractive engine of economic growth, enhancing public health and streamlining trade in the zone, Jordan and the region. texts.full_text: istributed A\* (HDA\*) distribute work among the processes using a global hash function. We analyze the search and communication overheads of state-of-the-art hash-based parallel best-first search algorithms, and show that although Zobrist hashing, the standard hash function used by HDA\*, achieves good load balance for many domains, it incurs significant communication overhead since almost all generated nodes are transferred to a different processor than their parents. We propose Abstract Zobrist hashing (AZH), an abstract hash function that generalizes Zobrist hashing and provides trading off between load balance and communication overhead. We show experimentally that AZH decreases the communication overhead of HDA\* by up to 90% without significantly

texts.full_text: egment has openings that allow for tissue ingrowth and endothelialization once implanted. However, these traditional stents may not fully conform to the curvature of some blood vessels due their fixed hinge points. To address this issue, a novel articulated intravascular stent is proposed in your invention disclosure: Invention: An improved self-expanding articulable stent comprises: - A series of connected segments arranged sequentially along an axis. Each segment has at least two articulation joints on either end that allow for independent movement around multiple axes (multi-axis articulation). The multi-articulation points enable the stent to adapt better and conform more closely with curved blood vessels, reducing potential stress concentration spots which can lead to fatigue failure or vessel wall injury. Furthermore: - The segments are preferably made of a shape memory alloy like Nitinol for self-expanding capabilities. - Each segment has openings allowing tissue ingrowth post-implantation.

Therefore, in order to kill tumor cells, it is inevitable to elevate the concentration of drugs inside tumor cells (Chen et al., [@CIT0002]). It is well known that tumor cells usually overexpress specific receptors on their membranes, which are potential targetsites for nanoparticles. By designing drug carriers with targeting ligands, the active targeting of cancer cells can be achieved (Liu et al., 2014). Targeting ligands on nano-carriers will bind to the corresponding receptors on tumor cells and induce endocytosis into tumor cells, thereby improving the concentration of drugs in tumor cells. Qantas CEO Alan Joyce had previously stated that moving Qantas's European hub from Singapore (where British Airways and its OneWorld partners provide services) to Dubai would allow his company greater flexibility, better connections through a single airport rather than multiple hubs around Southeast Asia or East Africa. By partnering with Emirates Airline which is already operating as an essential gateway for travelers between Europe/Africa/Middle-East destinations via the United Arab Emirate's hub at Terminal 3 in Al Maktoum International Airport (DXB), Qantas aims to streamline its services and enhance offerings. The ACCC’s preliminary approval of this alliance highlights a significant trust being placed upon these airlines by regulatory bodies, but with conditions. texts.full_text: s soon as possible. The basic principles of the Hollywood diet At the heart of the Hollywood diet is the rejection of carbohydrates and fats, with maximum protein intake, unsweetened fruits and vegetables that do not contain starch. The main rules: lack of breakfast; complete exclusion of alcohol and alcohol-containing products; daily consumption of pure still water in the amount of 1.5 liters. According to nutritionists, a lack of carbohydrates and a lack of breakfast can lead to serious health problems such as diabetes or hypoglycemia (low blood sugar). The body needs all macronutrients - proteins, fats and carbohydrates for its proper functioning: 1. Proteins: are the building blocks of our muscles; 2. Fats: provide energy storage and insulation around vital organs; 3. Carbohydrates: fuel your brain's activity throughout the day as they break down into glucose which is easily absorbed by cells in place where it’s needed most - including red blood cell production, central nervous system function or even during short-term physical exertion like sprinting! It should be noted that "lack of breakfast" isn't beneficial for weight loss and has been associated with increased risk factors related heart disease . Skipping meals leads people into binging later on which ends up putting more strain calorie wise than if one stuck closer their allotted amounts earlier throughout day/week etc... Also drinking enough water daily (1.5-2 liters) is important, however forcing yourself to drink this much might also be unnecessary as long you're paying attention thirst cues given body.. Instead of following fad diets like the Hollywood diet which can have negative side effects and aren't sustainable in a longer run; it's better focus on gradual lifestyle changes such creating calorie deficit through balanced eating habits coupled regular physical activity leading healthier , more manageable weight loss results over time

texts.full_text: tress. Because the shrinkage temperature is very close to the melting point, or in other words, the shutdown temperature, in the case of using the polyolefin porous film separator, it is necessary to prevent temperature increase in the battery by reducing the current immediately when the battery temperature reaches the shutdown temperature in the event of a charge failure or the like. If the pores do not close sufficiently, failing to reduce the current immediately, the battery temperature quickly rises to the separator's shrinkage temperature, causing a risk of thermal runaway due to internal short-circuiting. Various technical developments have been carried out to prevent short-circuiting caused by such separator thermal shrinkage so as to enhance battery reliability. For example, Patent Document 1 proposes an electrochemical device configured of a separator including a porous substrate with a good heat resistance, filler particles, and a resin component for securing the shutdown function. Also, Patent Document 2 proposes a technique to use, in a lithium ion secondary battery, a separator including a porous film in which secondary particles are bondeD by a binder, the secondary particles prepared through sintering or melt-recrystallization of a part of primary particles of a ceramic material having a band gap. Patent Document 1: WO 2006/62153A Patent Document 2: JP 2006-147569A For example, according to the technique disclosed in Patent Document 1, it is possible to provide a safe electrochemical device in which thermal runaway will not easily occur even in the event of overheating. As expected , the results have shown to be different in teIt seems like you are trying to determine the appropriate clustering variable for your Hausman Taylor estimation using Stata and `xtoverid`. Cluster selection is crucial because it can significantly affect your standard errors (S.E.). Here’s a more detailed approach on how to decide which variable to cluster by: 1. Theoretical Justification: Start with the theoretical justification for clustering. Commonly, researchers use time or geographical units if they suspect that observations within these clusters are not independent (e.g., firms in the same industry or regions). 2. Data Characteristics: Consider the characteristics of your data. For example: - If you have repeated measures over time for individuals, clustering by individual ID might be appropriate to account for within-individual correlation. - If observations are from different geographical areas or institutions, cluster by these variables if they may introduce common shocks. 3. Empirical Tests: Perform empirical tests such as the Breusch-Pagan test for heteroskedasticity and the Wooldridge test for autocorrelation to guide your decision on clustering. - For example, `xtest` can be used in Stata to check for serial correlation within panels. 4. Sensitivity Analysis: Run sensitivity analyses by trying different clustering variables and compare the results. This won’t tell you which is correct but will help understand how robust your estimates are to different specifications. Here's an example of how you might run these tests in Stata: ```stata xtset id time xtreg y x, cluster(id) vce(robust) estat icc ``` This will give you the intraclass correlation coefficients (ICC), which can help decide if clustering by `id` is appropriate. texts.full_text: e phenomena aren't derived by mathematical principles alone; they're grounded firstly in empirical evidence. Mathematics is a language, yes – it's one that allows us precise and efficient ways of expressing complex ideas about the physical universe but at its core physics deals with matter (and energy), motion or forces. These are not concepts defined within mathematics itself - rather math provides frameworks for understanding these things quantitatively once they've been observed. So, while I understand why you might think that Physics is dependent on Mathematics – because we use mathematical equations to describe physical phenomena and solve problems in physics often require a good grasp of algebra or calculus. But it's important not just see this as math being used by physicists; rather than mathematics providing the tools for understanding, but also recognizing where those 'tools' come from. texts.full_text: of a thread so that the thread gets dispatched preferentially over other threads in the system, to put the thread on a global run queue serviced by multiple central processing units (CPUs), and so forth. Also, the thread usually pins all its text and memory to avoid page faulting. Despite the preceding, there are several issues that can andoften do occur with this approach. Firstly, the Unix priority is a global value for an entire process rather than per-thread; thus it cannot be used effectively in multi-core systems where multiple threads within the same application may have different priorities. This means that even if one thread has high importance, all other processes will still compete equally on CPU resources with this single higher-priority thread. Secondly, the global run queue serviced by CPUs is a common resource shared among many applications and their respective low-latency threads; thus it cannot guarantee the priority of any particular application or its thread. This means that even if one's own process has high importance, other processes may still preempt this single higher-priority thread. Thirdly, the pinning technique can cause memory fragmentation over time as different parts are allocated and deallocated in a non-uniform manner; thus it cannot be used effectively for long-term applications. This means that even if one's own process has high importance, the system may still have to resort back into page faulting due to insufficient contiguous free space. In view of these issues, the present invention aims at providing an improved mechanism which allows low-latency scheduling on simultaneous multi-threaded cores without causing memory fragmentation or compromising fairness among different applications and their respective threads. Hansen is a charter member of SCGI and is widely considered to be the voice in the field of climate change. After 46 years in government service, Hansen stepped down from his position as director (for 31 years) of NASA's Goddard Institute for Space Studies in 2013. Jim has also served as an adjunct professor in the Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences at Columbia University. He continues his prolific research and writing on the topic of climate change and proposals for dealing with the problem effectively. After graduate school, Hansen continued his work with transfer models and attempting to understand the Venusian atmosphere. This naturally led to the same computer codes being used to understand the Earth's atmosphere. He used these codes to study the effects that aerosols and trace gases have on the climate. Hansen has also contributed to the further understanding of the Earth's climate through the development and use of global climate models. Hansen is best known for his research in the field of climatology, his testimony on climate change to congressional committees in 1988 that helped raise broad awareness of global warming, and his advocacy of action to limit the impacts of climate change. After Trump's Withdrawal from Paris, Nukes Are More Than Ever In the wake of President Donald Trump’s announcement that he was withdrawing the United States from the international climate agreement, governors from over a dozen states immediately announced they would take climate action on their own.
